articles.title: Gregory Bovino’s Final Days: Harsh Words and Few Regrets
articles.summary: He was the face of the Trump administration’s immigration crackdown. But as he begins a retirement that was not entirely voluntary, the Border Patrol leader says he did not go far enough.

articles.title: Kim vows to 'irreversibly' cement North Korea's nuclear status
articles.summary: In his speech, Kim expressed pride in the country's rapid expansion of nuclear weapons and missiles in recent years, calling it the "right" choice.

articles.title: ‘What If Donald Shouts at Me?’ Trump Sours on British Leader Over Iran War
articles.summary: President Trump once called Prime Minister Keir Starmer a friend. But Britain’s decision not to join the attacks on Iran has led to merciless mocking by the president.

articles.title: Asia boosts coal use as Iran war squeezes global LNG supplies
articles.summary: Analysts say coal may stabilize supplies for now but they warn that continued reliance on the polluting fuel will worsen air pollution.

articles.title: A New Phase of the War in Iran, and the Latest on the LaGuardia Plane Crash
articles.summary: Plus, how dancing the tango can help patients with Parkinson’s disease.

articles.title: When Trump Wants Something Done, He Dispatches ICE to Do It
articles.summary: President Trump has increasingly used Immigration and Customs Enforcement to push personal and political objectives, and on Monday sent agents to airports across the country to help deal with long sec

articles.title: College Graduates Are Facing the Grimmest Job Market in Years
articles.summary: Artificial intelligence could reshape work, but for now a low-hire, low-fire labor market is the main impediment for young people seeking employment.

articles.title: Denmark holds early elections spurred by Trump's threats to take Greenland
articles.summary: Denmark's prime minister called early parliamentary elections after gaining a popularity boost from standing up to President Trump over his threat to seize Greenland.

articles.title: Airstrikes may have destroyed Iran's last F-14s, ending a long, strange saga
articles.summary: The F-14 was made famous in Top Gun. The U.S. sold the planes to Iran in the 1970s, only for the two countries to become enemies. Iran kept its F-14s flying for decades in the face of U.S. sanctions.

articles.title: As parents age, their children face hard choices about when to take the car keys
articles.summary: States have many policies to stop risky older drivers from renewing their licenses. But in practice, it's often adult children who must decide when to take the car keys away from an aging parent.

articles.title: Trump delivers farmers another financial blow with Iran war
articles.summary: Some of President Trump's policies, the latest being the war in Iran, are testing his support among farmers who are being burdened with higher costs.

articles.title: ICE deployments created chaos for cities and cost them millions, NPR analysis finds
articles.summary: Local leaders report already-strapped police departments racked up overtime bills in the millions while others report a multi-million dollar hit to business during the worst ICE surges.

articles.title: Trump takes aim at windmills despite increasing energy costs
articles.summary: President Trump's mission to fight renewable wind energy comes at a time of rising energy costs.

articles.title: How weeks of war have reshaped Iran's negotiating position
articles.summary: How has Iran's negotiating position changed after weeks of war? NPR's A Martinez talks to Mohammad Ali Shabani, editor of the London-based news site Amwaj.media.

articles.title: British Army retires Land Rover vehicles from its fleet, marking the end of an era
articles.summary: The British Army has retired Land Rover vehicles from its fleet after 70 years of service.

articles.title: At least 66 killed in military plane crash in Colombia, head of armed forces says
articles.summary: Colombian officials say that a military cargo plane with 128 people on board, most of them soldiers, crashed shortly after taking off Monday in southwestern Colombia.

articles.title: UK police probe possible Iran link after Jewish charity ambulances set on fire
articles.summary: Police in London are investigating a suspected antisemitic hate crime attack after four vehicles belonging to a Jewish ambulance service were set on fire.

articles.title: In LaGuardia Crash That Killed 2, Call to ‘Stop!’ Came Too Late
articles.summary: A collision between an Air Canada Express jet and a fire truck on Sunday night left two pilots dead and dozens injured.

articles.title: Trump Delays Threat to Iran, but War Negotiations Are in Early Stage
articles.summary: President Trump postponed his threat to strike power plants in Iran, citing “productive conversations” with the Iranians. But officials said the talks were in an early stage and not substantive.

articles.title: Trump Faces Blowback on Easing Iran Oil Sanctions
articles.summary: President Trump once assailed the Obama administration for making cash payments to Iran. Now he supports sanctions relief that could give the country a $14 billion windfall.

articles.title: First female archbishop of Canterbury highlights progress and ongoing challenges
articles.summary: With the installation of the first female archbishop of Canterbury, women clergy in the Episcopal Church reflect on the struggle toward women's ordination and the future of women in ministry.

articles.title: Dominion still has pending lawsuits against election deniers such as Rudy Giuliani and Sidney Powell
